mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.5 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and imminent recession, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand collapse, forcing earnings downgrades and driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive returns dominate triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side momentum and capacity utilization recovery, triggering short-term equity rallies.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of recovery-phase observations, or the signal loses predictive accuracy outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings above 0.4 percent force expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in over 50 percent of high-inflation trigger months, or the correlation between inflation spikes and subsequent equity drawdowns falls below -0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment confirms structural labor market weakness, destroying earnings confidence and producing negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a Rank IC below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows, or positive 60-day returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly GDP growth confirms sustained aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, supporting higher equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of late-cycle expansion quarters, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year contraction in industrial production indicates a supply-side recession and falling capacity utilization, triggering defensive portfolio shifts and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in over 55 percent of contraction periods, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge in walk-forward validation.
